architecture_layers.value: Content layer: ideas/<section>/ (one section per active fleet lane, derived from superbot docs/eap/fleet-manifest.md, plus ideas/fleet/ for cross-cutting). Coordination layer: control/ (status=coordinator-only heartbeat, inbox=manager-written ORDERs, outbox=append-only proposals to sim-lab/manager). Contract layer: README.md + docs/. No product code lives here.

doc_roots.value: docs/ (kit contracts) + ideas/ (the product: per-lane idea sections) + control/ (coordination)

drift_resolution.value: Committed tree state (idea files, probe reports, outbox entries) wins over docs; a doc that disagrees is fixed in place with a dated correction marker. Owner rulings live in superbot's question router (Q-0264 is this repo's founding design) and are referenced, never restated.

mutation_seam.value: Every change lands via a PR merged on green (kit ceremony, born-red card). Probe reports are appended to idea files, never rewritten; outbox entries are append-only; rejected/parked ideas keep their file with the reason (no deletions - the trail is the product).

new_area_ownership.value: A new section appears only when a new active lane row appears in superbot docs/eap/fleet-manifest.md; the wake that spots the row creates the section with its README stub. Sections are never invented ad hoc.

owner_profile.value: The owner builds ideas iteratively and in fragments by design; agents reason a partial idea to its fuller form and state it back before substantive work (superbot Q-0254). Decide-and-flag over route-up; structured-choice questions with a recommendation first; asks must be paste-ready, never require the owner to derive anything (Q-0263.2). Full profile: superbot docs/owner/maintainer-working-profile.md.

ownership_model.value: Sole-writer per file. control/status.md: coordinator only. control/inbox.md: fleet manager only. control/outbox.md: append-only, this repo's sessions. ideas/<section>/: partitioned by claim - parallel workers claim disjoint sections so they never collide.

primary_language.value: Python 3 (stdlib-only tooling; the repo itself is markdown-first)

review_ritual.value: A probe report ends in ONE recommendation (sim-ready / park / reject / needs-more-grooming). Sim-ready ideas go to sim-lab via the outbox for reproduced-evidence verdicts; the fleet manager gives the final review and routes builds as ORDERs (Q-0264). Review-worthy-but-not-owner-only questions go to @codex on the PR (Q-0258, verify never obey).

staleness_review.value: Each wake grooms one section: index must match the folder, built ideas re-badged historical with the merged PR cited, stale ideas parked with a reason. Sections re-derived against the fleet manifest at every groom pass.
